---
title: "Staining mapping"
---

Two mapping modes exist for staining in `mappers.xlsx`. Which one to use depends
on the staining type.

→ For the mapper row structure and column layout, see [**mappers.xlsx**](reference-data-mappers.qmd).

## Choosing a mapping mode

| Staining type | Mapper row `Type` | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Standalone chemical stain | `procedure` | Entire stain mapped as one entity |
| IHC antibody stain | `list` | Each component mapped separately |
| In-situ hybridisation (ISH / CISH) | `list` | Each component mapped separately |
| Immunofluorescence (IF) | `list` | Each component mapped separately |

When a chemical stain is used as a **counterstain within an IHC panel**, it must
follow the `list` mode — not `procedure` — so it can be listed alongside the
antibody components.

## Chemical stains

Use `Type: procedure`. Map the entire staining procedure to its SNOMED CT
Concept ID (International Edition).

### Common SNOMED CT codes

| Stain | Concept ID | Fully Specified Name (FSN) |
|---|---|---|
| Hematoxylin & Eosin (H&E) | 104210008 | Hematoxylin and eosin stain method (procedure) |
| Alcian blue / PAS | 117224009 | Alcian blue with Periodic acid Schiff stain method (procedure) |
| Congo red | 117055006 | Congo red stain method (procedure) |
| Giemsa | 104208006 | Giemsa stain method (procedure) |
| Gomori | 117236003 | Gomori stain method (procedure) |
| Masson trichrome | 117018006 | Masson trichrome stain method (procedure) |
| Periodic acid Schiff (PAS) | 104216002 | Periodic acid Schiff stain method (procedure) |

### Finding a missing code

Use the [SNOMED CT Browser](https://browser.ihtsdotools.org/) (International
Edition). Search for the staining method name, select the result with semantic
type **procedure**, and copy the **Concept ID**. Use only **Active** concepts.

If no SNOMED CT code exists for a staining method, contact
[support](support.qmd) before leaving the field empty.

## Targeted stains (IHC, ISH, IF)

Use `Type: list`. Each individual staining component — each antibody or probe,
and the counterstain — must be a separate row that shares the same `Mapping Key`.

There are no SNOMED CT codes for IHC antibodies. Use a consistent free-text
string for the `Target` field (see [Antibody naming](#antibody-naming) below).

### Counterstain rule

Every targeted staining must include the counterstain as an additional `list`
row with `Stain Type: chemical`. Use the SNOMED CT code for hematoxylin as a
substance:

| Mapping Key | Type | Stain Type | Code | Scheme | Meaning |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| *(any IHC key)* | `list` | `chemical` | 12710003 | SNOMED CT | Hematoxylin stain (substance) |

Omitting the counterstain does not block export, but leaves the staining record
incomplete in BigPicture.

### Antibody naming {#antibody-naming}

Use a short string that pathologists and researchers would recognise from routine
practice. Apply the same string consistently across all datasets your site
submits — inconsistent naming for the same antibody across datasets reduces
searchability in the BigPicture Discovery service.

| Preferred | Avoid |
|---|---|
| `CD3` | `CD3 antibody`, `Anti-CD3` |
| `PD-L1` | `Programmed Death Ligand 1`, `Anti-PD-L1` |
| `PD-L1 (SP142)` | `Anti-PD-L1 (SP142)`, `SP142` alone |

A recognised clone designation is acceptable when used consistently.

## Controlled vocabulary

Valid values for the controlled fields in staining mapper rows.

### `Stain Type` (list rows only)

| Value | Use for |
|---|---|
| `chemical` | Chemical compounds (including counterstains) |
| `immunogenic` | Antibody-based stains (IHC, IF) |
| `in_situ_hybridisation` or `ish` | In-situ hybridisation (ISH / CISH) |

Values are matched case-insensitively.

### `Compound` (list rows only)

| Value | Use for |
|---|---|
| `ANTIBODY` | IHC or IF antibody |
| `Double-stranded DNA` | ISH / CISH probe (dsDNA) |
| `Single-stranded DNA` | ISH / CISH probe (ssDNA) |
| `RNA` | RNA FISH probe |
| `Synthetic oligonucleotide` | Synthetic probe |

### `Reporter Type`

| Value |
|---|
| `chromogen` |
| `fluorochrome` |

### `Reporter Color`

| Value |
|---|
| `brown` |
| `red` |
| `black` |
| `teal` |
| `purple` |
| `green` |
| `yellow` |
